highlights: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with 70,000+ hectares of lowland rainforest and 350+ bird species
Crocodile Lake (Bau Sau) hike: 5km trek past 400-year-old trees to spot critically endangered Siamese crocodiles
Early morning gibbon trek at 4:30 AM to hear yellow-cheeked gibbons and spot families swinging in canopy

thingsToKnow.crowds: Weekends are busier; gibbon tours limited to 4 spots per day—book months ahead. Night safari groups can be large (25+ people).
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Trails are generally easy to follow with signs. Crocodile Lake requires 5km hike each way. Overall accessible for moderate fitness levels.
thingsToKnow.safety: Leeches and mosquitoes are serious during rainy season (Jun–Nov); leech socks recommended. Some watchtowers need maintenance with missing steps. Be cautious hiking alone in jungle.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Ferry crossing required to enter park (60,000 VND). Bike rental available (150,000 VND per day). No ATMs in park—bring cash.
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Best time: December–May (dry season). Avoid June–November due to leeches and mud. Early morning visits best for wildlife spotting.

visitorTips: Book tours directly with guides, not through accommodation hosts who take large commissions affecting experience quality
Rent bikes inside the park; renting outside requires extra 100,000 VND entrance fee per river crossing
Bring leech socks, headlamp, binoculars, cash, water, and snacks; download AllTrails app for offline trail maps

faq.question: How do I get to Cat Tien National Park from Ho Chi Minh City?
faq.answer: Local bus 59 from near airport to Nga Tu Ga station (6,000 VND, 35–40 mins), then bus to park (110,000 VND, 5.5 hours). Or motorbike via QL20 highway. Ferry crossing to park costs 60,000 VND.

faq.question: Is the gibbon trek worth booking in advance?
faq.answer: Yes. Only 4 spots available daily at 4:30 AM start. Book months ahead. Cost ~1,000,000 VND ($40). Reviewers report seeing gibbon families within 20m—considered exceptional experience.

faq.question: What wildlife will I realistically see?
faq.answer: Gibbons, langurs, monkeys (multiple species), Siamese crocodiles at Bau Sau Lake, sambar deer, civets, owls, and 350+ bird species. Success depends on timing, patience, and attentive observation of treetops.

sentiment.positive: Travelers praise the raw jungle wilderness, abundant wildlife diversity, well-maintained trails with botanical signs, knowledgeable guides, reasonable entrance fees, and transformative nature experiences. Strong appeal for birders and wildlife enthusiasts.
sentiment.negative: Poor road maintenance to Crocodile Lake, deteriorating watchtowers with safety hazards, noise from tour trucks disturbing forest tranquility, expensive Crocodile Lake fee (250,000 VND) felt unjustified by some, night safari criticized for poor animal welfare practices and limited visibility for rear passengers, and some accommodation rooms had mold issues.
